CREATE TABLE date_test (id INTEGER, event_date DATE);
SELECT * FROM date_test WHERE event_date > DATE '2020-01-01';
SELECT * FROM date_test WHERE event_date < DATE '2023-01-01' AND event_date != '-infinity';
SELECT id FROM date_test WHERE event_date BETWEEN DATE '-infinity' AND DATE '2023-12-31';
SELECT event_date, COUNT(*) FROM date_test GROUP BY event_date HAVING event_date IS NOT NULL;
SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM date_test WHERE event_date IS NOT NULL) AS total_dates, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM date_test WHERE event_date = 'epoch') AS epoch_count;
SELECT dt1.id FROM date_test dt1 JOIN date_test dt2 ON dt1.event_date = dt2.event_date WHERE dt1.id != dt2.id;
SELECT MAX(event_date) AS latest_date FROM date_test WHERE event_date != 'infinity';
SELECT MIN(event_date) AS earliest_date FROM date_test WHERE event_date != '-infinity';
